content.text: The failures highlight a glob convention question. Standard glob: `*` does not cross `/`, so `*.test.*` only matches at root. I'll update the tests to use `**/*.test.*` for files in subdirectories — which is the standard convention.

content.text: Now the GitHub Actions workflow. It needs to accept fixture data — I'll have the workflow read fixtures from `fixtures/` directory (chosen via env var or default), run the script, and emit clearly-delimited output.

input.content:
name: PR Label Assigner

on:
  push:
  pull_request:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      fixture:
        description: 'Fixture name under fixtures/ to evaluate'
        required: false
        default: 'default'

permissions:
  contents: read

env:
  FIXTURE: ${{ github.event.inputs.fixture || 'default' }}

jobs:
  test:
    name: Pester unit tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Pester tests
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $cfg = New-PesterConfiguration
          $cfg.Run.Path = './PrLabelAssigner.Tests.ps1'
          $cfg.Run.Exit = $true
          $cfg.Output.Verbosity = 'Detailed'
          Invoke-Pester -Configuration $cfg

  assign:
    name: Assign labels
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Resolve fixture
        id: fixture
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $name = $env:FIXTURE
          if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($name)) { $name = 'default' }
          $dir = Join-Path 'fixtures' $name
          if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $dir)) {
            throw "Fixture directory not found: $dir"
          }
          $rules = Join-Path $dir 'rules.json'
          $files = Join-Path $dir 'files.json'
          if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $rules)) {
            $rules = './labels.config.json'
          }
          "rules=$rules" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT -Append
          "files=$files" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT -Append

      - name: Run label assigner
        shell: pwsh
        env:
          RULES: ${{ steps.fixture.outputs.rules }}
          FILES: ${{ steps.fixture.outputs.files }}
        run: |
          Write-Host "===PR-LABEL-ASSIGNER-BEGIN==="
          Write-Host "fixture=$env:FIXTURE"
          $labels = & ./Invoke-LabelAssigner.ps1 -RulesPath $env:RULES -FilesPath $env:FILES -Json
          Write-Host "labels=$labels"
          Write-Host "===PR-LABEL-ASSIGNER-END==="

content.content: File created successfully at: /home/passp/repos/GHA-bench/workspaces/2026-05-06_173435/12-pr-label-assigner/powershell-tool-opus47-1m-medium/.github/workflows/pr-label-assigner.yml (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

on:

  pull_request:
  workflow_dispatch:

        description: 'Fixture name under fixtures/ to evaluate'

permissions:

env:
  FIXTURE: ${{ github.event.inputs.fixture || 'default' }}

jobs:
  test:
    name: Pester unit tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Pester tests

  assign:
    name: Assign labels
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Resolve fixture
        id: fixture
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $name = $env:FIXTURE
          if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($name)) { $name = 'default' }
          $dir = Join-Path 'fixtures' $name
          if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $dir)) {
            throw "Fixture directory not found: $dir"
          }
          $rules = Join-Path $dir 'rules.json'
          $files = Join-Path $dir 'files.json'
          if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $rules)) {
            $rules = './labels.config.json'
          }
          "rules=$rules" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT -Append
          "files=$files" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT -Append

      - name: Run label assigner

content.text: I want to allow act to override the fixture per test. Let me adjust the workflow so the resolve step reads `$env:FIXTURE` directly (act's `--env` flag injects it).

jobs:

permissions:

env:

tool_use_result.originalFile:
name: PR Label Assigner

on:
  push:
  pull_request:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      fixture:
        description: 'Fixture name under fixtures/ to evaluate'
        required: false
        default: 'default'

permissions:
  contents: read

env:
  FIXTURE: ${{ github.event.inputs.fixture || 'default' }}

jobs:
  test:
    name: Pester unit tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Pester tests
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $cfg = New-PesterConfiguration
          $cfg.Run.Path = './PrLabelAssigner.Tests.ps1'
          $cfg.Run.Exit = $true
          $cfg.Output.Verbosity = 'Detailed'
          Invoke-Pester -Configuration $cfg

  assign:
    name: Assign labels
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Resolve fixture
        id: fixture
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $name = $env:FIXTURE
          if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($name)) { $name = 'default' }
          $dir = Join-Path 'fixtures' $name
          if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $dir)) {
            throw "Fixture directory not found: $dir"
          }
          $rules = Join-Path $dir 'rules.json'
          $files = Join-Path $dir 'files.json'
          if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $rules)) {
            $rules = './labels.config.json'
          }
          "rules=$rules" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT -Append
          "files=$files" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT -Append

      - name: Run label assigner
        shell: pwsh
        env:
          RULES: ${{ steps.fixture.outputs.rules }}
          FILES: ${{ steps.fixture.outputs.files }}
        run: |
          Write-Host "===PR-LABEL-ASSIGNER-BEGIN==="
          Write-Host "fixture=$env:FIXTURE"
          $labels = & ./Invoke-LabelAssigner.ps1 -RulesPath $env:RULES -FilesPath $env:FILES -Json
          Write-Host "labels=$labels"
          Write-Host "===PR-LABEL-ASSIGNER-END==="

input.new_string:

        id: fixture
        shell: pwsh
        env:
          INPUT_FIXTURE: ${{ github.event.inputs.fixture }}
        run: |
          # Prefer runner-injected FIXTURE (act --env), then workflow_dispatch input, then 'default'
          $name = $env:FIXTURE
          if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($name)) { $name = $env:INPUT_FIXTURE }
          if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($name)) { $name = 'default' }
          Write-Host "Using fixture: $name"
          $dir = Join-Path 'fixtures' $name

name: PR Label Assigner

on:
  push:
  pull_request:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      fixture:
        description: 'Fixture name under fixtures/ to evaluate'
        required: false
        default: 'default'

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  test:
    name: Pester unit tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run Pester tests
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          $cfg = New-PesterConfiguration
          $cfg.Run.Path = './PrLabelAssigner.Tests.ps1'
          $cfg.Run.Exit = $true
          $cfg.Output.Verbosity = 'Detailed'
          Invoke-Pester -Configuration $cfg

  assign:
    name: Assign labels
    needs: test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Resolve fixture
        id: fixture
        shell: pwsh
        env:
          INPUT_FIXTURE: ${{ github.event.inputs.fixture }}
        run: |
          # Prefer runner-injected FIXTURE (act --env), then workflow_dispatch input, then 'default'
          $name = $env:FIXTURE
          if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($name)) { $name = $env:INPUT_FIXTURE }
          if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($name)) { $name = 'default' }
          Write-Host "Using fixture: $name"
          $dir = Join-Path 'fixtures' $name
          if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $dir)) {
            throw "Fixture directory not found: $dir"
          }
          $rules = Join-Path $dir 'rules.json'
          $files = Join-Path $dir 'files.json'
          if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $rules)) {
            $rules = './labels.config.json'
          }
          "rules=$rules" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT -Append
          "files=$files" | Out-File -FilePath $env:GITHUB_OUTPUT -Append

      - name: Run label assigner
        shell: pwsh
        env:
          RULES: ${{ steps.fixture.outputs.rules }}
          FILES: ${{ steps.fixture.outputs.files }}
        run: |
          Write-Host "===PR-LABEL-ASSIGNER-BEGIN==="
          Write-Host "fixture=$env:FIXTURE"
          $labels = & ./Invoke-LabelAssigner.ps1 -RulesPath $env:RULES -FilesPath $env:FILES -Json
          Write-Host "labels=$labels"
          Write-Host "===PR-LABEL-ASSIGNER-END==="

content.text:
All 25 Pester tests pass; act ran all 3 fixture cases through the workflow successfully (used 2 of 3 act runs); `act-result.txt` is on disk.

**Summary of deliverables:**

- `PrLabelAssigner.psm1` — module with `Test-GlobMatch`, `Get-PrLabels`, `Import-LabelRules`, `Invoke-LabelAssigner` (standard glob: `*` non-recursive, `**` recursive)
- `PrLabelAssigner.Tests.ps1` — 15 unit tests built TDD-style (red → green)
- `Workflow.Tests.ps1` — 10 structure tests (YAML triggers/jobs/steps, file references, actionlint exit 0)
- `Invoke-LabelAssigner.ps1` — CLI wrapper (text or `-Json` output)
- `labels.config.json` — default rule set
- `fixtures/{default,docs-only,empty}/` — three test fixtures with known-good expected outputs
- `.github/workflows/pr-label-assigner.yml` — `push`/`pull_request`/`workflow_dispatch` triggers, `test` job runs Pester, `assign` job depends on it and runs the script; uses `actions/checkout@v4`, `shell: pwsh`
- `Run-ActTests.ps1` — harness that stages files into a temp git repo, runs `act push --rm` per fixture, asserts exit 0 + ≥2 "Job succeeded" + exact label arrays (`["api","tests","documentation"]`, `["documentation"]`, `[]`), appends all output to `act-result.txt`
- `act-result.txt` — captured output for all three cases
